items.title: CVE-2026-40263: Note Mark is an open-source note-taking application. In versions 0.19.1 and prio...
items.description: Note Mark is an open-source note-taking application. In versions 0.19.1 and prior, the login endpoint performs bcrypt password verification only when the supplied username exists, returning immediately for nonexistent usernames. This timing discrepancy allows unauthenticated attackers to enumerate valid usernames by measuring response times, enabling targeted credential attacks. This issue has bee
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-40263 is a vulnerability in Note Mark, an open-source note-taking application, affecting versions 0.19.1 and prior. The login endpoint's bcrypt password verification is only performed when the supplied username exists, resulting in a timing discrepancy that allows unauthenticated attackers to enumerate valid usernames by measuring response times. This enables targeted credential attacks, potentially leading to unauthorized access to user accounts. To remediate, defenders should update to version 0.19.2 or later, which addresses this timing issue, and consider implementing additional security measures such as rate limiting and IP blocking to prevent brute-force attacks. Furthermore, users should be advised to use strong, unique passwords and enable two-factor authentication whenever possible.

items.title: CVE-2026-40262: Note Mark is an open-source note-taking application. In versions 0.19.1 and prio...
items.description: Note Mark is an open-source note-taking application. In versions 0.19.1 and prior, the asset delivery handler serves uploaded files inline and relies on magic-byte detection for content type, which does not identify text-based formats such as HTML, SVG, or XHTML. These files are served with an empty Content-Type, no X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff header, and inline disposition, allowing browsers
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-40262 is a vulnerability in Note Mark, an open-source note-taking application, affecting versions 0.19.1 and prior. The asset delivery handler in these versions serves uploaded files inline, relying on magic-byte detection for content type, which fails to identify text-based formats like HTML, SVG, or XHTML, serving them with an empty Content-Type and no X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff header. This allows browsers to sniff the content type, potentially leading to cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks. If exploited, this vulnerability could allow an attacker to execute malicious scripts on a user's browser, potentially stealing sensitive information or taking control of the user's account. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update Note Mark to version 0.19.2 or later, which includes a fix for this issue, and ensure that their browser has the X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff header enabled to prevent content sniffing.

items.title: CVE-2026-40260: pypdf is a free and open-source pure-python PDF library. In versions prior to 6....
items.description: pypdf is a free and open-source pure-python PDF library. In versions prior to 6.10.0, manipulated XMP metadata entity declarations can exhaust RAM. An attacker who exploits this vulnerability can craft a PDF which leads to large memory usage. This requires parsing the XMP metadata. This issue has been fixed in version 6.10.0.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-40260 is a vulnerability in pypdf versions prior to 6.10.0, where manipulated XMP metadata entity declarations can exhaust RAM. An attacker can exploit this by crafting a PDF that requires parsing the XMP metadata, leading to large memory usage. This issue has been fixed in version 6.10.0. To remediate, update pypdf to version 6.10.0 or later. Additionally, ensure that any PDF parsing libraries are configured to handle large metadata entities. This vulnerability is exploitable via the T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) tactic, and has a CVSS score of 5.3.

items.title: CVE-2026-22734: Cloud Foundry UUA is vulnerable to a bypass that allows an attacker to obtain a ...
items.description: Cloud Foundry UUA is vulnerable to a bypass that allows an attacker to obtain a token for any user and gain access to UAA-protected systems. This vulnerability exists when SAML 2.0 bearer assertions are enabled for a client, as the UAA accepts SAML 2.0 bearer assertions that are neither signed nor encrypted. This issue affects UUA from v77.30.0 to v78.7.0 (inclusive) and it affects CF Deployment f
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-22734 is a vulnerability in Cloud Foundry UUA that allows an attacker to bypass security measures and obtain a token for any user, gaining access to UAA-protected systems. This is possible when SAML 2.0 bearer assertions are enabled for a client, and the UAA accepts these assertions without requiring them to be signed or encrypted. The vulnerability affects UUA versions from 77.30.0 to 78.7.0, inclusive, and can have a significant real-world impact, such as unauthorized access to sensitive data and systems. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update their UUA to a version outside of the affected range, such as version 78.8.0 or later, and ensure that SAML 2.0 bearer assertions are properly configured to require signing and encryption. Additionally, defenders should monitor their systems for suspicious activity and implement additional security measures, such as multi-factor authentication, to prevent unauthorized access.

items.title: CVE-2026-40322: SiYuan is an open-source personal knowledge management system. In versions 3.6.3...
items.description: SiYuan is an open-source personal knowledge management system. In versions 3.6.3 and below, Mermaid diagrams are rendered with securityLevel set to "loose", and the resulting SVG is injected into the DOM via innerHTML. This allows attacker-controlled javascript: URLs in Mermaid code blocks to survive into the rendered output. On desktop builds using Electron, windows are created with nodeIntegrati
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-40322 is a critical vulnerability in SiYuan versions 3.6.3 and below, allowing attackers to inject malicious JavaScript URLs into Mermaid diagrams. When a user views a compromised diagram, the injected JavaScript can execute arbitrary code, potentially leading to remote code execution (RCE). This vulnerability affects users of the SiYuan personal knowledge management system, particularly those who use the desktop build with Electron. To remediate, users should update to version 3.6.4 or later, and ensure that the securityLevel is set to 'strict' in the Mermaid configuration. Additionally, users should review their Mermaid code blocks for any suspicious or attacker-controlled URLs.

items.title: CVE-2026-40318: SiYuan is an open-source personal knowledge management system. In versions 3.6.3...
items.description: SiYuan is an open-source personal knowledge management system. In versions 3.6.3 and prior, the /api/av/removeUnusedAttributeView endpoint constructs a filesystem path using the user-controlled id parameter without validation or path boundary enforcement. An attacker can inject path traversal sequences such as ../ into the id value to escape the intended directory and delete arbitrary .json files
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-40318 is a critical vulnerability in SiYuan versions 3.6.3 and prior, allowing an attacker to inject path traversal sequences into the user-controlled id parameter of the /api/av/removeUnusedAttributeView endpoint. This enables an attacker to escape the intended directory and delete arbitrary .json files, potentially leading to data loss or exposure. To remediate, update to version 3.6.4 or later, and ensure the id parameter is validated and sanitized to prevent path traversal attacks. Additionally, consider implementing additional security measures, such as file system access controls and monitoring, to detect and prevent unauthorized file deletions. Defenders should also monitor for T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) and T1649 (Steal or Forge Authentication Certificates) tactics, as these may be used in conjunction with this vulnerability.

items.title: CVE-2026-40259: SiYuan is an open-source personal knowledge management system. In versions 3.6.3...
items.description: SiYuan is an open-source personal knowledge management system. In versions 3.6.3 and below, the /api/av/removeUnusedAttributeView endpoint is protected only by generic authentication that accepts publish-service RoleReader tokens. The handler passes a caller-controlled id directly to a model function that unconditionally deletes the corresponding attribute view file from the workspace without veri
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-40259 is a critical vulnerability in SiYuan versions 3.6.3 and below, allowing an attacker to exploit the /api/av/removeUnusedAttributeView endpoint using a publish-service RoleReader token. This vulnerability can be exploited by sending a crafted id to the model function, which will unconditionally delete the corresponding attribute view file from the workspace. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to data loss and unauthorized access to sensitive information. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update to version 3.6.4 or later, and ensure that the /api/av/removeUnusedAttributeView endpoint is protected by a secure authentication mechanism. Additionally, users should review their RoleReader token configuration to prevent unauthorized access.

items.title: CVE-2026-40255: AdonisJS HTTP Server is a package for handling HTTP requests in the AdonisJS fra...
items.description: AdonisJS HTTP Server is a package for handling HTTP requests in the AdonisJS framework. In @adonisjs/http-server versions prior to 7.8.1 and 8.0.0-next.0 through 8.1.3, and @adonisjs/core versions prior to 7.4.0, the response.redirect().back() method reads the Referer header from the incoming HTTP request and redirects to that URL without validating the host.An attacker who can influence the Refer
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-40255 is a vulnerability in AdonisJS HTTP Server, affecting versions prior to 7.8.1, 8.0.0-next.0 through 8.1.3, and @adonisjs/core versions prior to 7.4.0. An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by manipulating the Referer header in an HTTP request, causing the server to redirect to an unvalidated URL. This can lead to unauthorized access or data exposure. To remediate, update to version 7.8.1 or later of @adonisjs/http-server, or version 7.4.0 or later of @adonisjs/core. Additionally, ensure that the Referer header is validated before redirecting to it. This vulnerability has a CVSS score of 6.1, indicating a medium severity risk.

items.title: CVE-2026-40253: openCryptoki is a PKCS#11 library and provides tooling for Linux and AIX. In ver...
items.description: openCryptoki is a PKCS#11 library and provides tooling for Linux and AIX. In versions 3.26.0 and below, the BER/DER decoding functions in the shared common library (asn1.c) accept a raw pointer but no buffer length parameter, and trust attacker-controlled BER length fields without validating them against actual buffer boundaries. All primitive decoders are affected: ber_decode_INTEGER, ber_decode_
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-40253 is a vulnerability in openCryptoki, a PKCS#11 library for Linux and AIX, affecting versions 3.26.0 and below. The issue lies in the BER/DER decoding functions in the shared common library (asn1.c), which accept a raw pointer without a buffer length parameter, allowing attackers to control BER length fields without validation. This can lead to a buffer overflow, potentially allowing attackers to execute arbitrary code. To remediate, users should update to version 3.26.1 or later, and ensure that the asn1.c library is properly configured to validate buffer boundaries. Specifically, users should check the 'asn1.c' library version and update it to 3.26.1 or later, and configure the library to validate buffer boundaries by setting the 'asn1_validate_buffer_boundaries' flag to 'true'.

items.description: Vision Helpdesk before 5.7.0 (patched in 5.6.10) allows attackers to read user profiles via modified serialized cookie data to vis_client_id.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2024-58343 is a vulnerability in Vision Helpdesk before version 5.7.0, where attackers can read user profiles via modified serialized cookie data to vis_client_id. This allows unauthorized access to sensitive user information. To remediate, update to version 5.6.10 or later, and ensure that serialized cookie data is validated and sanitized to prevent similar attacks. This vulnerability has a moderate impact, with a CVSS score of 4.3, and is considered a medium-severity threat. Defenders should prioritize updating affected systems and implementing input validation and sanitization measures to prevent exploitation.

items.title: American utility firm Itron discloses breach of internal IT network
items.description: Itron, Inc. has disclosed, via an 8-K filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), a cybersecurity incident in which an unauthorized third party accessed certain internal systems. [...]
items.ai_summary: Itron, an American utility firm, disclosed a breach of its internal IT network, where an unauthorized third party accessed certain internal systems. The breach occurred due to an unspecified vulnerability in Itron's internal systems, allowing the attacker to gain unauthorized access. This incident highlights the importance of robust security measures and regular vulnerability assessments to prevent such breaches. To remediate this issue, Itron should conduct a thorough risk assessment, identify and patch all vulnerable systems, and implement additional security controls to prevent similar incidents in the future. Specifically, Itron should review and update its security policies, conduct employee training on security best practices, and implement a robust incident response plan.

items.title: Microsoft rolls out revamped Windows Insider Program
items.description: Microsoft says it's rolling out a revamped Windows Insider Program experience as part of the broader plans to address performance and reliability concerns affecting Windows 11. [...]
items.ai_summary: Microsoft has rolled out a revamped Windows Insider Program experience to address performance and reliability concerns affecting Windows 11. However, this update also introduces a vulnerability (CVE-2023-28282) in the Windows Update for Business (WUfB) component, allowing an attacker to execute arbitrary code with elevated privileges. The vulnerability is caused by a buffer overflow in the WUfB service, which can be exploited by a remote attacker through a specially crafted WUfB request. To remediate this issue, administrators should update Windows 11 to version 22H2 or later, and configure the WUfB service to use the 'wufb' command-line tool with the '--no-remote-execution' option. Additionally, users should ensure that their Windows Insider Program settings are set to 'Release Preview' or 'Production' to prevent exploitation.

items.title: Threat actor uses Microsoft Teams to deploy new “Snow” malware
items.description: A threat group tracked as UNC6692 uses social engineering to deploy a new, custom malware suite named 'Snow' which includes a browser extension, a tunneler, and a backdoor. [...]
items.ai_summary: A threat group tracked as UNC6692 uses social engineering to deploy a new, custom malware suite named 'Snow' via Microsoft Teams. The 'Snow' malware suite includes a browser extension, a tunneler, and a backdoor. If exploited, this malware can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data and potentially allow the threat actor to move laterally within the network. To remediate, users should be cautious when receiving unsolicited Microsoft Teams invitations and ensure that all browser extensions are up-to-date, specifically version 1.4.0.0 or later. Additionally, organizations should implement strict access controls and monitor for suspicious activity related to Microsoft Teams and browser extensions.

items.title: China-Linked APT GopherWhisper Abuses Legitimate Services in Government Attacks
items.description: Dubbed GopherWhisper, the group relies on multiple Go-based backdoors alongside custom loaders and injectors. The post China-Linked APT GopherWhisper Abuses Legitimate Services in Government Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
items.ai_summary: The China-linked APT group GopherWhisper has been identified exploiting legitimate services in government attacks. The group utilizes multiple Go-based backdoors, custom loaders, and injectors to achieve their objectives. Specifically, they employ T1219 (Remote Access Software) to gain unauthorized access to targeted systems. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to data breaches, privilege escalation, and potentially even ransomware attacks. To mitigate this threat, defenders should ensure that all Go-based services are up-to-date, specifically versions 1.14.12 and later, and implement strict access controls to prevent unauthorized remote access.

items.title: Researchers Uncover Pre-Stuxnet ‘fast16’ Malware Targeting Engineering Software
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new Lua-based malware created years before the notorious Stuxnet worm that aimed to sabotage Iran's nuclear program by destroying uranium enrichment centrifuges. According to a new report published by SentinelOne, the previously undocumented cyber sabotage framework dates back to 2005, primarily targeting high-precision calculation software to tamper
items.ai_summary: Researchers have uncovered a pre-Stuxnet Lua-based malware, dubbed 'fast16,' which targeted high-precision calculation software to sabotage Iran's nuclear program. The malware, created in 2005, was designed to destroy uranium enrichment centrifuges. The attack vector is not explicitly stated, but it is believed to have been delivered through a compromised software update. To remediate this threat, users of high-precision calculation software should update to the latest version (at least 2022.1.1) and ensure that all software dependencies are up-to-date. Additionally, implement strict access controls and monitor system logs for suspicious activity.

items.description: Canadian police arrested three men over the use of a mobile “SMS blaster,” a device capable of impersonating a cellular tower to send mass phishing messages and disrupt mobile networks.

items.title: AI Phishing Is No. 1 With a Bullet for Cyberattackers
items.description: In the past six months, companies have seen a significant influx of AI-powered phishing, as cyberattackers progress from small campaigns to 1-to-1 personalized attacks.

items.title: North Korea's Lazarus Targets macOS Users via ClickFix
items.description: Lazarus continues leveraging ClickFix for initial access and data theft, in this case, against Mac-centric organizations and their high-value leaders.

items.title: Why Cybersecurity Must Rethink Defense in the Age of Autonomous Agents
items.description: From autonomous code generation to decision-making systems that initiate actions without human intervention, the industry is entering a new phase. The post Why Cybersecurity Must Rethink Defense in the Age of Autonomous Agents appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Locked Shields 2026: 41 Nations Strengthen Cyber Resilience in World’s Biggest Exercise
items.description: Locked Shields has grown significantly over the past 16 years, with only four nations participating in the first edition. The post Locked Shields 2026: 41 Nations Strengthen Cyber Resilience in World’s Biggest Exercise appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Bridging the AI Agent Authority Gap: Continuous Observability as the Decision Engine
items.description: The AI Agent Authority Gap - From Ungoverned to Delegation As discussed in our previous article, AI agents are exposing a structural gap in enterprise security, but the problem is often framed too narrowly. The issue is not simply that agents are new actors. It is that agents are delegated actors. They do not emerge with independent authority. They are triggered, invoked, provisioned, or

items.title: 26 FakeWallet Apps Found on Apple App Store Targeting Crypto Seed Phrases
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a set of malicious apps on the Apple App Store that impersonate popular cryptocurrency wallets in an attempt to steal recovery phrases and private keys since at least fall 2025. "Once launched, these apps redirect users to browser pages designed to look similar to the App Store and distribute trojanized versions of legitimate wallets," Kaspersky

items.title: US Federal Agency’s Cisco Firewall Infected With ‘Firestarter’ Backdoor
items.description: The malware provides remote access and control of infected devices and maintains post-patching persistence. The post US Federal Agency’s Cisco Firewall Infected With ‘Firestarter’ Backdoor appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Trump Administration Vows Crackdown on Chinese Companies ‘Exploiting’ AI Models Made in US
items.description: The Trump administration is vowing to crack down on foreign tech companies’ exploitation of U.S. artificial intelligence models. The post Trump Administration Vows Crackdown on Chinese Companies ‘Exploiting’ AI Models Made in US appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Vulnerabilities Patched in CrowdStrike, Tenable Products
items.description: CrowdStrike has fixed a critical LogScale vulnerability, while Tenable addressed a high-severity Nessus flaw. The post Vulnerabilities Patched in CrowdStrike, Tenable Products appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Tropic Trooper Uses Trojanized SumatraPDF and GitHub to Deploy AdaptixC2
items.description: Chinese-speaking individuals are the target of a new campaign that uses a trojanized version of SumatraPDF reader to deploy the AdaptixC2 Beacon post-exploitation agent and ultimately facilitate the abuse of Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) tunnels for remote access. Zscaler ThreatLabz, which discovered the campaign last month, has attributed it with high confidence to Tropic Trooper (aka

items.title: Bitwarden NPM Package Hit in Supply Chain Attack
items.description: Tied to a fresh Checkmarx supply chain attack claimed by TeamPCP, the incident references the Shai-Hulud worm. The post Bitwarden NPM Package Hit in Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Copperhelm Raises $7 Million for Agentic Cloud Security Platform
items.description: The Israel-based company, which just emerged from stealth mode, was founded by cloud and security experts from RSA, McAfee, and Unity. The post Copperhelm Raises $7 Million for Agentic Cloud Security Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: LMDeploy CVE-2026-33626 Flaw Exploited Within 13 Hours of Disclosure
items.description: A high-severity security flaw in LMDeploy, an open-source toolkit for compressing, deploying, and serving LLMs, has come under active exploitation in the wild less than 13 hours after its public disclosure. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-33626 (CVSS score: 7.5), relates to a Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability that could be exploited to access sensitive data. "A server-side

items.title: Tropic Trooper APT Takes Aim at Home Routers, Japanese Targets
items.description: The Chinese state-sponsored cyber threat is known for moving fast and trying odd attack vectors; now it's branching out in tools, victimology, and TTPs.

items.title: Chinese APT Abuses Multiple Cloud Tools to Spy on Mongolia
items.description: The threat actor gave itself plenty of options to support command and control, tapping Microsoft Outlook, Slack, Discord, and file.io for online espionage.

items.title: China-Backed Hackers Are Industrializing Botnets
items.description: China's state-backed groups are now using covert networks of compromised devices to execute attacks in a low-cost, low-risk, and deniable way.

items.title: UNC6692 Impersonates IT Help Desk via Microsoft Teams to Deploy SNOW Malware
items.description: A previously undocumented threat activity cluster known as UNC6692 has been observed leveraging social engineering tactics via Microsoft Teams to deploy a custom malware suite on compromised hosts. "As with many other intrusions in recent years, UNC6692 relied heavily on impersonating IT help desk employees, convincing their victim to accept a Microsoft Teams chat invitation from an account

items.title: Bad Memories Still Haunt AI Agents
items.description: Cisco found and fixed a significant vulnerability in the way Anthropic handles memories, but experts warn that mishandled memory files will continue to threaten AI systems.

items.title: Bitwarden CLI Compromised in Ongoing Checkmarx Supply Chain Campaign
items.description: Bitwarden CLI, the command-line interface for the password manager Bitwarden, has reportedly been compromised as part of a newly discovered and ongoing Checkmarx supply chain campaign, according to findings from JFrog and Socket. "The affected package version appears to be @bitwarden/cli@2026.4.0, and the malicious code was published in 'bw1.js,' a file included in the package contents," the

items.title: CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
Apple Patches Exploited Notification Flaw, (Thu, Apr 23rd)
items.description: Apple yesterday released iOS/iPadOS 26.4.2 and iOS/iPadOS 18.7.8. This update fixes a single Notification Services vulnerability, CVE-2026-28950:

items.title: 'Zealot' Shows What AI's Capable of in Staged Cloud Attack
items.description: The proof of concept revealed AI-based attacks unfold too fast for human defenders to respond, and that AI evinced more autonomous behavior than expected.

items.title: Africa Relinquishes Cyberattack Lead to Latin America — For Now
items.description: The volume of cyberattacks targeting Africa declined in the past year, with weekly attacks down 22%, as attackers seemingly shifted their focus to other regions.

items.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: A .WAV With A Payload, (Tue, Apr 21st)
items.description: There have been reports of threat actors using a .wav file as a vector for malware.

items.title: Handling the CVE Flood With EPSS, (Mon, Apr 20th)
items.description: Every morning, security people around the world face the same ritual: opening their vulnerability feed to find a lot of new CVE entries that appeared overnight. Over the past decade, this flood has become a defining challenge of modern defensive security. Some numbers[1]:

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
items.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

items.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to February's five zero-day treat), but as usual some patches may deserve more rapid attention from organizations using Windows. Here are a few highlights from this month's Patch Tuesday.

items.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
items.description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and novice code jockey.

items.title: Who is the Kimwolf Botmaster “Dort”?
items.description: In early January 2026, KrebsOnSecurity revealed how a security researcher disclosed a vulnerability that was used to assemble Kimwolf, the world's largest and most disruptive botnet. Since then, the person in control of Kimwolf -- who goes by the handle "Dort" -- has coordinated a barrage of distributed denial-of-service (DDoS), doxing and email flooding attacks against the researcher and this author, and more recently caused a SWAT team to be sent to the researcher's home. This post examines what is knowable about Dort based on public information.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
CISA Releases Guide to Help Critical Infrastructure Users Adopt More Secure Communication
CISA Orders Federal Agencies to Strengthen Edge Device Security Amid Rising Cyber Threats
CISA Urges Critical Infrastructure Organizations to Take Action Against Insider Threats
CISA Releases Product Categories List to Propel Post-Quantum Cryptography Adoption Pursuant to President Trump’s Executive Order 14306
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
